summary: Interdisciplinary degree combining computer science, information systems, and business administration, currently in the 8th and final semester.
details: Core focus on software engineering, databases, algorithms, and distributed systems
Currently writing my bachelor thesis on a domain-specific language for BPMN process modeling
The thesis is published open source and generates executable .bpmn files for Camunda and Operaton engines
8th semester, thesis phase, targeted graduation in 2026

thesis.title: A Domain-Specific Language for Executable BPMN
thesis.summary: Developing a DSL that lets developers describe business processes in a concise, code-first notation and compile them to executable BPMN files compatible with Camunda and Operaton process engines. The project is published as open source.

summary: Full academic year abroad in San Diego, California as part of my B.Sc. program, focused on computer science theory, algorithms, and advanced programming concepts.
details: Coursework in Algorithms & Data Structures, Advanced Programming Languages, and Software Systems
Deepened understanding of formal language theory, type systems, and compiler construction
Studied at an international research university, broadened perspective on engineering approaches and academic culture

institution: Bildungszentrum für Technik und Gestaltung, Oldenburg
summary: German university entrance qualification from a technical high school with an IT specialization. Final grade 1.3 (German scale, 1.0 best; approx. 4.0 GPA).
details: Advanced classes: Information Technology, Mathematics, English
IT coursework covered graphical programming in C#, microcontroller development, computer fundamentals, binary logic, and electrical engineering basics
Took Information Processing and Business/Economics as part of the Abitur
Final grade 1.3 on the German scale (1.0 is highest; roughly equivalent to a 4.0 GPA)

summary: Planned Master's program in Business Informatics, to begin after bachelor graduation.
